commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server yolo auto-approval helper, passes that flag through main and side-thread approval handling, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +82. Total +111 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source-level, yes: current main routes supported app-server approvals through requestPluginApproval even when the runtime tuple is yolo, but no live app-server yolo run is posted.
commands.review_followup: The contributor should repair the permission-approval scope and add live behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over an external PR that still lacks after-fix real setup proof.

target.title: fix(codex): honor yolo app-server approval policy
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes a Codex app-server approval regression where OpenClaw could still open plugin approval prompts even when the runtime is already configured for full yolo mode (`approvalPolicy: "never"` with `sandbox: "danger-full-access"`).

The patch:

- Adds a shared `shouldAutoApproveCodexAppServerApprovals()` policy helper.
- Auto-accepts app-server command and file approval requests only when the effective runtime policy is full yolo.
- Keeps OpenClaw's before-tool-call policy hook in the path before any auto-approval decision.
- Applies the same policy to side-thread approval handling while respecting the side thread binding's effective approval policy and sandbox.
- Adds regression coverage for command approvals, file approvals, config policy boundaries, main-thread routing, and side-thread routing.

## Review Notes

Codex review found one issue before PR submission: side-thread approval routing originally checked the default app-server runtime instead of the persisted side-thread binding. That could auto-approve a side-thread approval even when the bound thread policy was `on-request` or the sandbox was not `danger-full-access`.

I fixed that before opening this PR. The side-question path now computes the effective `approvalPolicy` and `sandbox` from the binding first, then falls back to app-server defaults.

No remaining blocking issues found in the reviewed diff.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts -t "forwards side-thread command approvals"`
  - Red/green checked: failed before the side-thread binding fix, passed after.
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`
  - Passed outside the sandbox: 322 tests across 4 files.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
  - Passed outside the sandbox.

Note: the combined Vitest command was also tried inside the Codex sandbox first. It hit `listen EPERM` on tests that bind `127.0.0.1`, so I reran the same command outside the sandbox for the authoritative result.
target.author: earlvanze
